# LP Grapher

> Interactive MATH 466 Linear Programming learning for KNUST Optimization II — graphing tool, calculator, step-by-step solver, and 21 topic lessons with practice questions.

## Learn

- [Learn Hub](https://example.com/learn): Browse all MATH 466 topics by module with progress tracking.

- [Introduction to Linear Programming](https://example.com/learn/intro-to-linear-programming): What LP is, what “programming” means, and when models are linear.
- [LP Definitions: Feasible & Optimal](https://example.com/learn/lp-definitions): Objective, constraints, feasible solutions, optimal solutions, and the feasible region.
- [General LP Formulation](https://example.com/learn/general-lp-formulation): Standard mathematical form with n decision variables and m constraints.
- [Key Terminology](https://example.com/learn/key-terminology): Coefficients cj, technological coefficients aij, and resource availabilities bi.
- [Graphing Linear Inequalities](https://example.com/learn/graphing-linear-inequalities): Boundary lines, test points, and half-plane shading for ax + by ≤ c.
- [Systems of Linear Inequalities](https://example.com/learn/systems-of-inequalities): Intersecting half-planes to find the feasible region of a system.
- [Problem Formulation](https://example.com/learn/problem-formulation): Four-step modeling: decision variables, data, constraints, objective.
- [Extreme Points & Optimal Solution](https://example.com/learn/extreme-points): Theorem: for bounded feasible regions, an optimum occurs at an extreme point.
- [Isoprofit / Isocost Line Method](https://example.com/learn/isoprofit-method): Slide parallel objective lines to find the optimal corner point.
- [Graphical Solution Procedure](https://example.com/learn/graphical-solution): End-to-end graphical solution for two-variable LP problems.
- [Standard Form & Slack Variables](https://example.com/learn/standard-form): Convert inequalities to equalities using slack, surplus, and artificial variables.
- [Simplex Algorithm](https://example.com/learn/simplex-algorithm): Pivot from basic feasible solution to optimum via cj − zj optimality test.
- [Special Cases: Unbounded & Infeasible](https://example.com/learn/special-cases): Detecting empty feasible regions and objectives that grow without bound.
- [Degeneracy](https://example.com/learn/degeneracy): Ties in leaving variable selection and zero basic variables at corners.
- [Artificial Variables](https://example.com/learn/artificial-variables): Introducing artificial variables to obtain an initial basic feasible solution.
- [Two-Phase Method](https://example.com/learn/two-phase-method): Phase I minimizes artificial variables; Phase II optimizes the true objective.
- [Duality Theory](https://example.com/learn/duality-theory): Primal-dual relationships and the duality theorem.
- [Sensitivity Analysis](https://example.com/learn/sensitivity-analysis): Post-optimality ranges for objective coefficients and RHS values.
- [Transportation Models](https://example.com/learn/transportation-models): Balanced transportation LP: supplies, demands, and unit costs.
- [Transshipment Models](https://example.com/learn/transshipment-models): Extension of transportation allowing intermediate transshipment nodes.
- [Assignment Models](https://example.com/learn/assignment-models): One-to-one assignment with Hungarian / opportunity cost method.

## Tools

- [Home](https://example.com/): Landing page with LP graphing, scientific calculator, and step solver in the tools dock.

## About

- [GitHub](https://github.com/samuel6814/LP_graphing_lightweight): Source code and project repository.
- Course: MATH 466 Optimization II (KNUST).
